Critical: A fully successful Plaid Link sandbox enrollment ends with a "Pay by Bank is unavailable" banner on checkout. See step 13 below, and §4 P1 in the report further down.
Self-contained report — every screenshot is embedded as base64. Use the toggle below to switch between viewports. Generated 2026-05-05 13:55 UTC.

Linear user journey · 14 steps

Each step shows the captured screenshot for the selected viewport plus any issues that fire at that step. Toggle viewports below.

Pay-by-Bank Checkout — UX Audit Report

Site: Liberty (Bensalem) storefront on dutchie-fullstack-pbb VAPE

Run date: 2026-05-04 · headless Chromium · guest user, pickup

Viewports: iPhone SE 375×548 · iPhone 13 390×664 · iPhone 14 Pro 430×740 · Desktop 1440×900

Driver: [run.mjs](./run.mjs) · screenshots in screenshots/ · per-step JSON in observations/

§1 Executive summary

The Pay-by-Bank flow is discoverable and functional across all four target viewports. The cart drawer surfaces PBB as a co-equal CTA next to "Proceed to checkout", and the checkout page elevates PBB above the legacy "Pay in-store" option with a clear Pay now (faster) · $0 Fee toggle and an in-line bank picker.

Critical finding: a fully successful Plaid Link sandbox enrollment ends in a Pay by Bank is unavailable. Please use a different method of payment. banner on checkout — with the embedded bank picker still rendered below it asking the user to pick a bank again. Reproducible at both mobile and desktop. See §4 P1.

The strongest UX wins are at the decision moment (cart drawer) and at commit (checkout right rail with sticky Order Total + Place Order). The weakest moments are:

  • iPhone SE (375×548) is materially compromised — the storefront's login nudge consumes the only above-fold CTA real estate, and 0 product-action CTAs are visible without scrolling.
  • The mobile checkout (390+) requires deep scrolling through Account → Type → Time → Medical → Summary → Payment before the user encounters PBB. A guest user has to traverse ~6 sections before they see the PBB picker, despite having opted into PBB in the cart drawer.
  • Two dev-mode runtime errors were captured during direct route navigation; one (costBreakdown undefined in the checkout analytics monitor) is a real null-safety bug worth fixing.

    • Severity rubric: Critical = blocks order placement or hides PBB on a target viewport. Major = significant friction, drop-off risk, or a11y violation. Minor = polish/copy.

    §2 Top 5 mobile issues

    #SeverityWhereIssue
    M1MajorCart drawer (390×664)"Before You Go" header renders with empty content area below it (see screenshots/mobile-walkthrough/04-cart-drawer-top.png). Looks like a broken upsell module. Either populate or hide.
    M2MajorCheckout (390×664, 430×740)PBB picker is the last of 6+ collapsible sections. A user who selected "Pay by bank instantly" in the cart drawer should land on the checkout already-anchored to or with the PBB section pre-expanded; today they scroll past Account, Type, Time, Medical, Summary first. (07-checkout-page-bottom.png)
    M3MajorCheckout payment selectorThe radio toggle copy "Pay now (faster) · $0 Fee" vs "Pay in-store · Cash only" is a strong nudge — but the Cash only badge sits inside the unselected option, so users may miss it until they're at the register. Surface that earlier (e.g., on the PDP add-to-cart confirmation).
    M4MajorPDP / storefrontThe "Login for recommendations…" popover anchored to the cart icon overlaps the cart count badge on 390 and obscures it entirely on 375. This is the primary gating control for entering checkout. (02-pdp shots)
    M5MinorStorefront banner copy" ALL ITEMS WILL CHARGED AS THE PRICE LISTED AT THE TIME OF PURCHASE " — grammar/typo ("WILL CHARGED"). Visible at all mobile viewports. (storefront-bottom.png)

    §3 Top 5 desktop issues

    #SeverityWhereIssue
    D1MajorCheckout right rail (1440)Right-rail order summary contains 5 stacked promotional/operational paragraphs ("All promotions are restricted…", "Download the Liberty Wallet…", "Avoid the ATM Fees…") between the dispensary header and Subtotal. This pushes the Place Order CTA below the form's Account section vertical center on first paint. (07-checkout-page-top.png) Consider collapsing into an info disclosure.
    D2MinorCheckout summary"Special Instructions" link styled as a bare green + Special Instructions — looks like an icon-text affordance but is a plain link. Inconsistent with the standard form-field treatment used everywhere else.
    D3MinorCheckout PBB sectionLink your bank to pay instantly heading shows a horizontal scroller of bank logos (Wells Fargo, Capital One, Chase, US Bank, Citi…). On 1440 it's clipped on the right edge — only 5 logos fit and there's no visual scroll affordance. (checkout-bottom.png)
    D4MajorPlace Order button contextThe Place Order CTA appears in the right rail before the user has selected a payment method in the form (the radio defaults to "Pay now (faster)"). Clicking Place Order without filling Time / Phone / Birthdate must produce a validation explosion — worth a follow-up to verify the error state is friendly.
    D5MinorCart drawer (1440)At desktop the cart "drawer" still renders as a right-side panel, but the underlying page beneath it shows skeleton blocks instead of the previous PDP. (cart-top.png) Background should remain the PDP for context.

    §4 Plaid Link assessment

    Plaid Link sandbox was driven end-to-end at both mobile (390×664) and desktop (1440×900) using First Platypus Bank (user_good/pass_good, no OAuth popup). Captures in screenshots/{mobile,desktop}-plaid-walkthrough/.

    Step-by-step capture

    #ScreenSource
    04Cart drawer "Pay by bank instantly" CTA — entry pointapp
    05–06DutchiePay account creation form (email → name/DOB/phone/password) at /pay/signupDutchie consumer
    09Plaid Link iframe loaded (cdn.plaid.com/link/v2/stable/link.html?...token=link-sandbox-...)Plaid sandbox
    10aPlaid consent: "Use Plaid to pay by bank at Dutchie" → ContinuePlaid
    10Institution search → "First Platypus Bank" matched 3 sub-institutionsPlaid
    10aaSub-institution disambiguation (non-OAuth / OAuth / OAuth App2App)Plaid
    10b"Log in at First Platypus Bank — After logging in… check all these boxes" data-share consentPlaid
    11Credentials formPlaid
    12Account select — Plaid Checking 0000 / Plaid Saving 1111Plaid
    13"Syncing to your bank" interim loader (custom Dutchie screen, not Plaid)Dutchie
    14Return to checkout — observed banner: "Pay by Bank is unavailable. Please use a different method of payment."Dutchie

    Findings

    P1 — Critical: Successful Plaid enrollment ends in "Pay by Bank is unavailable" banner. Both viewports completed the full Plaid sandbox flow — credentials accepted, account selected, success returned to the app — and landed back on /stores/liberty-bensalem/checkout with the banner "Pay by Bank is unavailable. Please use a different method of payment." Yet the checkout below the banner still renders the embedded "Pay now (faster) · $0 Fee" toggle as the selected default and shows a fresh "Search for your bank" picker with Navy Federal/Wells Fargo/USAA/Chase/Capital One/Truist/Bank of America/Citibank/US Bank logos — i.e. the UI is asking the user to enroll a bank again, while the banner tells them it's unavailable. Conflicting state. Evidence: mobile-plaid-walkthrough/14-final-checkout-state-{top,bottom}.png, observation finalBody.

    This is reproducible across both viewports. The constellation has MockFraudAnalysis=true and the seed enabled PBB on Liberty Bensalem, so the failure is downstream of fraud and dispensary eligibility — likely either (a) the in-app finalization mutation failed silently while Plaid sandbox returned success, or (b) a missing prerequisite (KYC, address, account type compatibility) is being enforced after enrollment. Either way the user-facing UX is broken: a green "successful enrollment" handoff produces a red "unavailable" message.

    P2 — Major: Bank disambiguation is jarring. "First Platypus Bank" search yields 3 results (First Platypus Bank, First Platypus Bank - OAuth, First Platypus Bank - OAuth App2App). Real-world banks like Chase have the same pattern (the ↗ icon next to "Continue to login" indicates OAuth popup). For users on real Chase/Wells Fargo accounts that surface multiple sub-institutions, the disambiguation page has no help text explaining why there are three options or which one to pick. The dispensary-context user is unlikely to know whether to choose OAuth vs. credentials. Evidence: mobile-plaid-walkthrough/10-plaid-search-bank-top.png.

    P3 — Major: "Pay by Bank" wordmark in the page header overlaps the user email at narrow viewports. During step 5/6 of the signup flow, the persistent header reads Pay by Bankpbb-audit+1777936...@dutchie.com with no separator/wrap, visible at iPhone 13 (390) and SE (375). The email and PBB wordmark collide. Evidence: top crop of every screenshot under mobile-plaid-walkthrough/05- through 13- shows Pay by Bankpbb- cut off at the top of the iframe overlay.

    P4 — Minor: Plaid sandbox banner ("You are currently in Sandbox mode") is visible to end users. The dev consumer build with PLAID_ENV=sandbox shows this footer on every Plaid screen. Expected in this constellation but flagging as a sanity check — production must use PLAID_ENV=production to avoid leaking sandbox UI.

    P5 — Minor: "Syncing to your bank" interim loader is the only branded Dutchie screen in the chain. It appears between Plaid success and checkout return. It works, but the lock-icon + bank-icon-with-dots animation is the only DutchiePay-branded post-Plaid touchpoint — feels light vs. the heavy Plaid-branded sequence that preceded it. Consider a stronger confirmation card ("✓ Connected to First Platypus Bank checking •0000") before sending the user back to checkout, so they know enrollment succeeded.

    Entry / In-flow / Exit / Error recovery

  • Entry: Cart drawer green CTA + checkout's "Pay now (faster)" radio + bank logo carousel are the three entry points. All three lead to the same /pay/signup flow that hands off to Plaid Link. Strong discovery, no friction.
  • In-flow: Plaid sandbox cleanly handles search → consent → credentials → account select. Only awkward moment is the disambiguation page (P2). MFA was not triggered for First Platypus Bank — it would need to be probed by selecting a different sandbox institution (Plaid sandbox includes Tartan Bank for MFA testing).
  • Exit: Not exercised. The X/close icon is present in the top-right of every Plaid screen; cancellation behavior would need a separate audit run that clicks it at each step and confirms the user lands on a sensible recovery state.
  • Error recovery: The "Pay by Bank is unavailable" banner from P1 is the error-recovery UX for the most common failure mode here, and it's failing — it doesn't explain why, doesn't offer a retry, and doesn't tell the user what to do next. The cart drawer's PBB button is the only path back, and clicking it would re-enter the same broken loop.

    §6 Positive findings

  • Cart drawer is the strongest PBB moment: side-by-side green CTAs, "What is Pay by Bank?" disclosure link, dispensary + pickup type confirmed in one glance. Carries through identically across iPhone SE / 13 / 14 Pro — responsive design is solid here.
  • Checkout payment selector correctly nudges PBB with (faster) and $0 Fee framing. The selected-state styling (green border + filled radio) is unambiguous.
  • In-checkout bank search ("Search 12k+ banks" with logo carousel) is a smart embedded-search pattern that avoids a Plaid Link iframe round-trip on common banks.
  • Order summary right rail (desktop) stays visible while scrolling the form — sticky Place Order is a real conversion win.
  • Pickup time picker opens inline ("Select a pickup time…" → "Please select a time…" dropdown + Save) instead of a modal — appropriate for mobile.
  • Storefront → PDP → cart drawer flow is fast and reliable across all four viewports; Add-to-cart confirmation is immediate, no spinner state.
